verses.latin_vulgate: An extrahere poteris Leviathan hamo,  et fune ligabis linguam ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּמְשֹׁ֣ךְ לִוְיָתָ֣ן בְּחַכָּ֑ה וּ֝בְחֶ֗בֶל תַּשְׁקִ֥יעַ לְשֹׁנֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid pones circulum in naribus ejus,  aut armilla perforabis maxillam ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲתָשִׂ֣ים אַגְמֹ֣ן בְּאַפּ֑וֹ וּ֝בְח֗וֹחַ תִּקֹּ֥ב לֶחֱיֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid multiplicabit ad te preces,  aut loquetur tibi mollia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיַרְבֶּ֣ה אֵ֭לֶיךָ תַּחֲנוּנִ֑ים אִם־יְדַבֵּ֖ר אֵלֶ֣יךָ רַכּֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid feriet tecum pactum,  et accipies eum servum sempiternum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיִכְרֹ֣ת בְּרִ֣ית עִמָּ֑ךְ תִּ֝קָּחֶ֗נּוּ לְעֶ֣בֶד עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid illudes ei quasi avi,  aut ligabis eum ancillis tuis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽתְשַׂחֶק־בּ֭וֹ כַּצִּפּ֑וֹר וְ֝תִקְשְׁרֶ֗נּוּ לְנַעֲרוֹתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?

verses.latin_vulgate: Concident eum amici ?  divident illum negotiatores ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִכְר֣וּ עָ֭לָיו חַבָּרִ֑ים יֶ֝חֱצ֗וּהוּ בֵּ֣ין כְּֽנַעֲנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid implebis sagenas pelle ejus,  et gurgustium piscium capite illius ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽתְמַלֵּ֣א בְשֻׂכּ֣וֹת עוֹר֑וֹ וּבְצִלְצַ֖ל דָּגִ֣ים רֹאשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?

verses.latin_vulgate: Pone super eum manum tuam :  memento belli, nec ultra addas loqui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׂים־עָלָ֥יו כַּפֶּ֑ךָ זְכֹ֥ר מִ֝לְחָמָ֗ה אַל־תּוֹסַֽף׃
verses.king_james: Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce spes ejus frustrabitur eum,  et videntibus cunctis præcipitabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵן־תֹּחַלְתּ֥וֹ נִכְזָ֑בָה הֲגַ֖ם אֶל־מַרְאָ֣יו יֻטָֽל׃
verses.king_james: Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non quasi crudelis suscitabo eum :  quis enim resistere potest vultui meo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־אַ֭כְזָר כִּ֣י יְעוּרֶ֑נּוּ וּמִ֥י ה֝֗וּא לְפָנַ֥י יִתְיַצָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis ante dedit mihi, ut reddam ei ?  omnia quæ sub cælo sunt, mea sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֣י הִ֭קְדִּימַנִי וַאֲשַׁלֵּ֑ם תַּ֖חַת כׇּל־הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם לִי־הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: Who hath prevented me, that I should repay him? whatsoever is under the whole heaven is mine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non parcam ei, et verbis potentibus,  et ad deprecandum compositis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (לא) [לוֹ־]אַחֲרִ֥ישׁ בַּדָּ֑יו וּדְבַר־גְּ֝בוּר֗וֹת וְחִ֣ין עֶרְכּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis revelabit faciem indumenti ejus ?  et in medium oris ejus quis intrabit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־גִ֭לָּה פְּנֵ֣י לְבוּשׁ֑וֹ בְּכֶ֥פֶל רִ֝סְנ֗וֹ מִ֣י יָבֽוֹא׃
verses.king_james: Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?

verses.latin_vulgate: Portas vultus ejus quis aperiet ?  per gyrum dentium ejus formido.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דַּלְתֵ֣י פָ֭נָיו מִ֣י פִתֵּ֑חַ סְבִיב֖וֹת שִׁנָּ֣יו אֵימָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corpus illius quasi scuta fusilia,  compactum squamis se prementibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֭אֲוָה אֲפִיקֵ֣י מָגִנִּ֑ים סָ֝ג֗וּר חוֹתָ֥ם צָֽר׃
verses.king_james: His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Una uni conjungitur,  et ne spiraculum quidem incedit per eas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶחָ֣ד בְּאֶחָ֣ד יִגַּ֑שׁוּ וְ֝ר֗וּחַ לֹא־יָבֹ֥א בֵינֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Una alteri adhærebit,  et tenentes se nequaquam separabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִישׁ־בְּאָחִ֥יהוּ יְדֻבָּ֑קוּ יִ֝תְלַכְּד֗וּ וְלֹ֣א יִתְפָּרָֽדוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sternutatio ejus splendor ignis,  et oculi ejus ut palpebræ diluculi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲֽ֭טִישֹׁתָיו תָּ֣הֶל א֑וֹר וְ֝עֵינָ֗יו כְּעַפְעַפֵּי־שָֽׁחַר׃
verses.king_james: By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

verses.latin_vulgate: De ore ejus lampades procedunt,  sicut tædæ ignis accensæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭פִּיו לַפִּידִ֣ים יַהֲלֹ֑כוּ כִּיד֥וֹדֵי אֵ֝֗שׁ יִתְמַלָּֽטוּ׃
verses.king_james: Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.

verses.latin_vulgate: De naribus ejus procedit fumus,  sicut ollæ succensæ atque ferventis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭נְּחִירָיו יֵצֵ֣א עָשָׁ֑ן כְּד֖וּד נָפ֣וּחַ וְאַגְמֹֽן׃
verses.king_james: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Halitus ejus prunas ardere facit,  et flamma de ore ejus egreditur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֭פְשׁוֹ גֶּחָלִ֣ים תְּלַהֵ֑ט וְ֝לַ֗הַב מִפִּ֥יו יֵצֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: In collo ejus morabitur fortitudo,  et faciem ejus præcedit egestas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽ֭צַוָּארוֹ יָלִ֣ין עֹ֑ז וּ֝לְפָנָ֗יו תָּד֥וּץ דְּאָבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Membra carnium ejus cohærentia sibi :  mittet contra eum fulmina, et ad locum alium non ferentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַפְּלֵ֣י בְשָׂר֣וֹ דָבֵ֑קוּ יָצ֥וּק עָ֝לָ֗יו בַּל־יִמּֽוֹט׃
verses.king_james: The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor ejus indurabitur tamquam lapis,  et stringetur quasi malleatoris incus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִ֭בּוֹ יָצ֣וּק כְּמוֹ־אָ֑בֶן וְ֝יָצ֗וּק כְּפֶ֣לַח תַּחְתִּֽית׃
verses.king_james: His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum sublatus fuerit, timebunt angeli,  et territi purgabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭שֵּׂתוֹ יָג֣וּרוּ אֵלִ֑ים מִ֝שְּׁבָרִ֗ים יִתְחַטָּֽאוּ׃
verses.king_james: When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum apprehenderit eum gladius, subsistere non poterit,  neque hasta, neque thorax :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשִּׂיגֵ֣הוּ חֶ֭רֶב בְּלִ֣י תָק֑וּם חֲנִ֖ית מַסָּ֣ע וְשִׁרְיָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.

verses.latin_vulgate: reputabit enim quasi paleas ferrum,  et quasi lignum putridum æs.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַחְשֹׁ֣ב לְתֶ֣בֶן בַּרְזֶ֑ל לְעֵ֖ץ רִקָּב֣וֹן נְחוּשָֽׁה׃
verses.king_james: He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non fugabit eum vir sagittarius :  in stipulam versi sunt ei lapides fundæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יַבְרִיחֶ֥נּוּ בֶן־קָ֑שֶׁת לְ֝קַ֗שׁ נֶהְפְּכוּ־ל֥וֹ אַבְנֵי־קָֽלַע׃
verses.king_james: The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi stipulam æstimabit malleum,  et deridebit vibrantem hastam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭קַשׁ נֶחְשְׁב֣וּ תוֹתָ֑ח וְ֝יִשְׂחַ֗ק לְרַ֣עַשׁ כִּידֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sub ipso erunt radii solis,  et sternet sibi aurum quasi lutum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֭חְתָּיו חַדּ֣וּדֵי חָ֑רֶשׂ יִרְפַּ֖ד חָר֣וּץ עֲלֵי־טִֽיט׃
verses.king_james: Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fervescere faciet quasi ollam profundum mare,  et ponet quasi cum unguenta bulliunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַרְתִּ֣יחַ כַּסִּ֣יר מְצוּלָ֑ה יָ֝֗ם יָשִׂ֥ים כַּמֶּרְקָחָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post eum lucebit semita :  æstimabit abyssum quasi senescentem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭חֲרָיו יָאִ֣יר נָתִ֑יב יַחְשֹׁ֖ב תְּה֣וֹם לְשֵׂיבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est super terram potestas quæ comparetur ei,  qui factus est ut nullum timeret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵֽין־עַל־עָפָ֥ר מׇשְׁל֑וֹ הֶ֝עָשׂ֗וּ לִבְלִי־חָֽת׃
verses.king_james: Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omne sublime videt :  ipse est rex super universos filios superbiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵֽת־כׇּל־גָּבֹ֥הַּ יִרְאֶ֑ה ה֝֗וּא מֶ֣לֶךְ עַל־כׇּל־בְּנֵי־שָֽׁחַץ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
